product_overview: FUD.ai is an API-first, agent-to-agent crypto sentiment oracle that analyzes FUD, manipulation, and rug-pull risk from on-chain, market, and social data. It returns structured verdict JSON for trading bots, while exposing only a read-only landing page and live dashboard for human visitors.
core_goals: Provide a pay-per-call analysis API for DeFi trading bots via CROO
Ingest on-chain, market, and social data to produce actionable verdicts
Use a 3-engine LLM pipeline to control costs and improve task specialization
Support semantic caching to reduce repeated inference cost
Deliver explainable outputs with evidence chains and confidence scoring
Provide a read-only landing page, pricing, API docs, and live dashboard
Maintain resilience under external API failures and LLM parsing issues

features.name: FUD Analysis Agent API
features.description: Primary API entry point for submitting a token for FUD analysis and receiving a structured verdict JSON.
user_flows: POST /api/agent with coin_symbol, optional contract_address, and optional chain_id -> Receive 200 with request_id, drama_index, branch_probabilities, evidence_chain, and executable_verdict -> Client uses verdict JSON for downstream trading or monitoring
POST /api/agent with coin_symbol but missing required coin_symbol field -> Receive 400 with an error message indicating the missing parameter
POST /api/agent for a token request when external data sources degrade -> Receive 200 with fallback=true and a reduced-quality verdict JSON
POST /api/agent for a repeated request matching a cached token window -> Receive 200 with served_from_cache=true and the cached verdict payload

features.name: Granular Dispatcher
features.description: Determines how an incoming token request should be routed based on symbol, contract address, and chain context, selecting the correct ingestion strategy.
user_flows: POST /api/agent with a Solana-style request and no contract_address -> Dispatcher selects RugCheck-first/native-token handling -> Pipeline proceeds with native-safe defaults and returns a verdict JSON
POST /api/agent with an EVM token contract_address and chain_id -> Dispatcher selects GoPlus and DexScreener priority -> Pipeline ingests security and liquidity data before reasoning
POST /api/agent with a native token symbol only -> Dispatcher selects Bybit order book and skips DEX/security fetches -> API returns 200 using native-token mode
POST /api/agent with malformed or incomplete token context -> Dispatcher cannot derive a valid ingestion strategy -> API returns 400 or a degraded fallback response depending on validation stage

features.name: Parallel Ingestion Layer
features.description: Concurrently collects market, security, social, and macro data from external APIs and scrapers before reasoning begins.
user_flows: POST /api/agent with a valid token request -> Ingestion layer calls Bybit, DexScreener, GoPlus, RugCheck, CoinGecko, DefiLlama, Twitter, and Telegram sources in parallel -> Aggregated snapshots are forwarded to the reasoning pipeline
POST /api/agent for a native token request -> Ingestion layer skips DEX/security fetches and returns safe defaults -> Pipeline completes with reduced source coverage
POST /api/agent when one or more external APIs rate limit or timeout -> Ingestion layer degrades gracefully and continues with remaining sources -> API returns 200 with fallback=true
POST /api/agent when a social source returns no usable content -> Ingestion layer returns empty or partial social payloads -> Reasoning continues using available on-chain data

features.name: Lightweight LLM Noise Filter
features.description: Uses the lightweight LLM engine to extract structured FUD claims from raw social text and normalize noisy social content into claim strings.
user_flows: POST /api/agent with ingested Twitter and Telegram text -> Noise filter calls the lightweight engine -> Receive a JSON array of claim strings that can be used by the heavyweight reasoning engine
POST /api/agent with social text that fails JSON parsing in the lightweight step -> Noise filter falls back to an empty claim array -> Pipeline continues without crashing
POST /api/agent with minimal or no social content -> Noise filter returns an empty or near-empty claim list -> Heavyweight reasoning proceeds using on-chain and market data
POST /api/agent when lightweight engine output is malformed -> safe parsing rejects the payload -> System uses fallback handling and returns a degraded but valid verdict response

features.name: DeepSeek MCTS Evaluator with ReAct
features.description: Runs the heavyweight reasoning stage across dynamic branches, optionally requesting more data once through a ReAct loop, then produces the final executable verdict JSON.
user_flows: POST /api/agent after noise-filtered claims are available -> Heavyweight engine evaluates scenario branches and may request FETCH_MORE once -> Pipeline returns drama_index, dominant_branch, confidence, and executable_verdict

POST /api/agent when the evaluator determines more perp or DEX evidence is needed -> System performs one extra ReAct iteration to fetch additional data -> Final verdict is generated after the extra evidence arrives
POST /api/agent when the heavyweight engine outputs malformed JSON -> safeParseJSON rejects the response -> Pipeline returns the fallback verdict with fallback=true
POST /api/agent with completed reasoning and valid structured output -> API returns 200 with evidence_chain and branch_probabilities for downstream consumption

features.name: Semantic Caching
features.description: Avoids repeat inference for the same coin within a short time window by returning previously computed verdicts from cache.
user_flows: POST /api/agent for a coin that was analyzed recently -> Cache lookup succeeds -> API returns the cached verdict with served_from_cache=true
POST /api/agent for a coin outside the cache TTL window -> Cache lookup misses -> Full ingestion and reasoning pipeline runs normally

POST /api/agent for the same coin within 5 minutes but with changing market conditions -> Cache still returns the prior verdict by design -> Client receives a cost-saving but potentially stale response
POST /api/agent when cache infrastructure is unavailable -> Cache lookup fails -> Pipeline bypasses cache and completes analysis through the live flow

features.name: Read-only Landing Page and Live Dashboard
features.description: Provides public-facing documentation, pricing information, CROO integration context, and a live dashboard that displays analysis results without any chatbot interaction.
user_flows: GET landing page routes for documentation and pricing -> Receive rendered Next.js pages with API docs, CROO pricing context, and examples -> User views static, read-only product information
GET live dashboard route -> Receive current drama index, scenario tree, and evidence chain visuals -> Dashboard displays analysis state without editing or chat input
GET live dashboard on a mobile device -> Receive responsive layout optimized for high-resolution mobile screens -> Dashboard content remains readable without overflow
GET dashboard or public pages when backend data is unavailable -> Page still renders shell content and existing snapshots where possible -> Users see degraded but usable read-only UI

tech_stack: DeepSeek LLM (heavyweight MCTS evaluator via OpenRouter)
CoinGecko REST API (market data, macro metrics)

features.name: FUD Analysis Agent API
features.description: Main entry point that accepts a POST request with coin_symbol, contract_address, and chain_id. Runs the full MCTS pipeline (Dispatcher → Ingestion → LLM noise filter → DeepSeek MCTS evaluator with ReAct loop) and returns a structured FUD verdict JSON.

endpoints.description: Submit a token for FUD analysis. Returns a verdict with drama_index, dominant_branch, branch_probabilities, evidence_chain, executable_verdict, served_from_cache, request_id, coin_symbol.

body.contract_address: string (optional, omit for native tokens like SOL/BTC)

features.name: Granular Dispatcher
features.description: Analyzes the incoming token request (symbol, contract_address, chain_id) and determines the optimal ingestion strategy — which APIs to call, what parameters to pass. Differentiates Solana tokens (no chain_id, RugCheck-first), EVM tokens (GoPlus + DexScreener), native tokens (Bybit order book only), and large-cap coins (CoinGecko + DefiLlama priority).

features.name: Parallel Ingestion Layer
features.description: Fires all data sources concurrently using Promise.all: Bybit order book, DexScreener DEX data, GoPlus security scan, RugCheck score, Twitter social intel (via RapidAPI), Telegram scraper (via Cheerio), CoinGecko markets, CoinGecko macro, DefiLlama TVL. Native tokens skip DEX/security fetches and return safe defaults.

features.name: Lightweight LLM Noise Filter (Step B)
features.description: Calls GPT-OSS (lightweight engine via OpenRouter) to extract structured FUD claims from raw Twitter + Telegram text. Outputs a JSON array of claim strings. Falls back to empty array on parse failure.

features.name: DeepSeek MCTS Evaluator with ReAct (Steps C + D)
features.description: Calls DeepSeek heavyweight engine to perform MCTS tree evaluation across dynamic branches. Can request FETCH_MORE (ReAct loop, max 1 extra iteration) for perp/DEX data. Returns Final Verdict JSON with drama_index, dominant_branch, branch_probabilities, evidence_chain, executable_verdict, confidence.

known_limitations.issue: Missing contract_address triggers 400 only for coin_symbol; contract_address is optional by design
known_limitations.impact: Requests without contract_address succeed and default to native token mode

known_limitations.issue: External API rate limits (GoPlus, RugCheck, DexScreener, Bybit) can cause fallback responses
known_limitations.impact: Pipeline may degrade to fallback=true with limited data quality

known_limitations.issue: Redis semantic caching not yet implemented
known_limitations.impact: served_from_cache is always false; no deduplication of identical requests

known_limitations.issue: DeepSeek LLM may occasionally output malformed JSON
known_limitations.impact: Handled by safeParseJSON + FALLBACK_VERDICT; pipeline does not crash
